principles: A measure is not a lever. 'read' detects the condition; 'action' is the only thing that moves. The two are never the same field.
Effect magnitude and evidence grade are separate axes. They are never multiplied into one number and never summed into a score or index.
This file holds population priors only: an expected-effect band and an evidence grade. Patient-specific attribution (is it HER driver?) is computed per patient and is deliberately not stored here.
Effect magnitudes are coarse bands flagged status:first-pass, pending the empirical-verification pass (Appendix B). They are not point estimates.
Gate priority (1-6) and dial headroom are the order we TRY things, not a scored leaderboard.
Instruments (RHR, HRV, skin-temp) are corroborators, never levers — so they are not in this library by design.

read.instrument: PHQ-9 item 9 ≥1, or any disclosed ideation
read.trigger: evaluated on EVERY administration, independent of total direction

actions.action: Immediate risk assessment + in-product crisis resource + handoff to a named human. Supersedes the normal flow; never ranked on the effect×confidence plane.
actions.honesty: Rare-fire, run-on-everyone safety layer. The item-9 read rides outside the PHQ-9 outcome trend so a falling total can never mask it.

read.instrument: Red-flag cluster (weight loss, fever/night sweats, lymphadenopathy, new severe/progressive fatigue, exertional dyspnea/chest symptoms, post-viral, adrenal-insufficiency cluster)

actions.action: Eject to medical evaluation first. The model does not sit on an occult cancer, cardiac disease, or adrenal insufficiency.
actions.honesty: When suicidality also fires, suicide risk is handled first; the red-flag evaluation proceeds in parallel.

read.trigger: PHQ-2 ≥2 / GAD-2 ≥2 (sensitive cutoff), confirmed on full scale

actions.confirmation: clinical response + re-snapshot; within-person PHQ-9 trend (total minus item 9)
actions.action: Treat (therapy and/or pharmacotherapy). Priority one by a wide margin: when present it dominates and suppresses every dial. No behavioral lever substitutes.
actions.honesty: PHQ-9 is the severity/response gauge, NOT the mood PRO. A partially-treated mood gate stays priority-one and keeps blocking dial attribution.

read.instrument: Medication / iatrogenic review (beta-blockers, sedating antihistamines, opioids, benzodiazepines, gabapentinoids, some antidepressants/antipsychotics/antihypertensives)

actions.action: Deprescribe or switch the offending agent.
actions.honesty: The cheapest, highest-yield reversible cause — more so because a positive mood gate can itself lead to prescribing a sedating agent.

actions.action: Route to care. A use disorder is a dominant lever no behavioral dial substitutes for — distinct from the moderate-alcohol drain (a dial).
actions.honesty: Serialize: treat the use disorder before crediting any sleep or mood dial change.

actions.action: Levothyroxine.
actions.honesty: Subclinical hypothyroidism is explicitly NOT a lever — RCTs show no reliable energy/QoL benefit from treating it. Asymptomatic incidental is out of scope (risk models own it).

actions.action: Iron repletion.
actions.honesty: Benefit concentrated at low ferritin, strongest in iron-deficient women; not a lever in the iron-replete. Asymptomatic incidental deferred to the risk models (no double-count).

read.trigger: out of range (elevated risk: metformin/PPI users, vegetarians, older adults)

actions.action: Replete B12 (± folate).
actions.honesty: Cheap, treatable, and sometimes irreversible if missed — the asymmetry justifies testing.

read.trigger: out of range on a poor subjective read

actions.action: Treat the cause; unmask occult renal/hepatic disease via chem/LFT.
actions.honesty: Read only to unmask a felt complaint. Asymptomatic management is the metabolic model's job — a value flagged here is not re-managed or double-scored.

actions.confirmation: CPAP adherence (nights, hours) + device residual AHI + Epworth; felt energy/body the real target
actions.action: CPAP / therapy. Upstream of the sleep dial — untreated it makes the sleep dial nearly futile, so it is a gate.
actions.honesty: STOP-Bang is a one-time screen (static inputs), never re-run as a tracker. Repeat sleep study only on treatment failure.

actions.confirmation: clinical response + re-snapshot; within-person PEG-3 trend
actions.action: Route to a pain pathway. Severe pain behaves like a dominant lever.
actions.honesty: PEG-3 is the pain-severity gauge within the body dimension, distinct from the body PRO (SSS-8 / PHQ-15). Gate-vs-dial is a severity cut: disabling pain is a gate, low-grade pain a dial-side drain.

read.instrument: Androgen-specific symptoms + 2× AM fasting testosterone (Endocrine Society workup) — referred out, no in-model panel
read.trigger: androgen-specific symptoms (low libido, ED, loss of morning erections) AFTER gates 1-5 cleared

actions.action: Refer to endocrinology. The model does not test or prescribe; it sets the felt-energy expectation honestly as small.
actions.honesty: Conditional gate. Does NOT fire on fatigue or a lab alone, on age-related decline without symptoms, or on a 'Low T'-clinic presentation. TTrials vitality endpoint was null (P=0.30).
actions.source: Snyder TTrials NEJM 2016; Bhasin Endocrine Society 2018

read.instrument: Symptom cluster + age/cycle history (NO hormone panel — not FSH/estradiol)
read.trigger: transitional symptoms in women ~40 to early 50s

actions.action: Acts THROUGH the mood and sleep gates, not beside them (no double-count). Estradiol-based MHT for vasomotor-mediated sleep disruption; estradiol as a first/co-first-line option for depression in EARLY perimenopause — never replacing standard depression care or the suicidality screen.
actions.honesty: Context flag, not a co-equal gate. Raises suspicion on the mood/sleep gates and routes to menopause-specific care. Proper home is the v2 managed-chronic gate type.
actions.source: Cintron 2016 (MHT sleep w/ vasomotor); Gordon 2018 (estradiol prevents early-perimenopausal depression); NAMS 2022

actions.confirmation: n-of-1: daily metric vs a baseline set before the change
actions.action: Regularize sleep/wake times — regularity is the lever, not just duration. Aim for a stable window and adequate length.
actions.honesty: Futile while an untreated OSA gate is upstream — gate before dial.
actions.source: Sleep Regularity Index (magnitudes in verification backlog)

actions.confirmation: n-of-1: daily metric vs a baseline set before the change
actions.action: Gentle activity on most days. Deliberately NOT the zone-based healthspan dose — low intensity is sufficient; the felt-energy benefit does not require a fitness-training dose.
actions.honesty: Effect sizes from the ~81-RCT exercise meta-analysis are in the verification backlog.
actions.source: Puetz 2006/2008; exercise-vitality meta-analysis (~81 RCTs, to verify)

actions.confirmation: n-of-1: daily metric vs a baseline set before the change
actions.action: At least one meaningful interaction most days; loneliness is the pole to watch. Structured contact.
actions.honesty: Strong but confounded evidence, so a default lever, not a precise effect. May be structurally constrained (socioeconomic) — non-response can mean the lever couldn't be applied, not that it failed.

actions.confirmation: n-of-1 with remove-and-reintroduce (reversible dial): watch the metric slide back on reintroduction
actions.action: Reduce alcohol. Small individually, cheap to fix, compounding through sleep and circadian timing.
actions.honesty: Distinct from the gate-level alcohol-use disorder. A reversible dial, so its effect can be confirmed by relaxing it.

actions.action: No late-day caffeine. Compounds through sleep quality.
actions.honesty: A reversible dial — confirm by reintroduction and watching the metric.

actions.confirmation: n-of-1 vs a baseline set before the change
actions.action: Morning light and time outdoors. Works through circadian timing.
actions.honesty: Small, cheap, compounding through sleep and circadian alignment.

read.trigger: persistent low-grade pain below the disabling threshold

actions.confirmation: n-of-1 vs a baseline set before the change
actions.action: Don't leave low-grade pain untreated.
actions.honesty: Dial-side drain, distinct from the disabling-pain gate (pain_pathway) — a severity cut per §2.1.
